summary.boundary: Derived claim-level synthesis from existing Paper 2 tables; no new DEM, CFD or experimental evidence.

rows.mechanism_claim: Increasing Stokes drag-to-weight ratio shifts the debris cloud downstream and increases weak breakthrough.
rows.manuscript_use: Supports the drive-state result and the statement that Fd/W is a compact migration coordinate.
rows.boundary: Representative final states only; not a full velocity-size parameter sweep.

rows.mechanism_claim: Increasing dimensionless debris inventory raises local sub-voxel blockage and the pressure-free structural index.
rows.manuscript_use: Supports the loading-scan result without implying a critical transition.
rows.boundary: Three single-seed loading states; pressure term is proxy-free and not pressure calibrated.

rows.mechanism_claim: Weak outlet breakthrough and local deposition occur without measurable outlet-connected pore-skeleton loss in the analyzed window.
rows.primary_coordinate: BTC, Bmax and C_loss considered jointly
rows.source_cases: low_drive_no_breakthrough; intermediate_drive_weak_breakthrough; high_drive_stronger_breakthrough; N=3000; N=6000; N=10000
rows.manuscript_use: Supports the central claim that breakthrough is a transport signal, not direct evidence of pore-network clogging.
rows.boundary: Pre-clogging window only; no universal critical clogging transition is identified.
